title: Qontrol Weekly CEO Report
subtitle: Executive quality snapshot for Apr 13 - Apr 19

laggingTeam.headline: MO is carrying the heaviest backlog.
laggingTeam.reason: MO currently owns 97 open tickets, including 20 high-severity items and 94 overdue follow-ups. That queue scores 203 points above the peer median on the executive backlog index.

narrativeCards.title: December spike has eased
narrativeCards.body: Weekly incidents peaked around Dec 1 and have since settled into a lower run rate. This gives leadership a cleaner baseline heading into the next supplier and production review. Rework activity in the briefing context is now concentrated in 2026-03-16, 2026-03-23, 2026-03-30, 2026-04-13, which supports a narrower improvement push.

narrativeCards.title: Supplier-change signal is improving
narrativeCards.body: Using the current portfolio plus seeded history, supplier-linked issues are trending down versus the January baseline after the component-source change implemented roughly three months ago.

narrativeCards.title: Backlog concentration needs attention
narrativeCards.body: MO currently owns 97 open tickets, including 20 high-severity items and 94 overdue follow-ups. That queue scores 203 points above the peer median on the executive backlog index.
